header.location: Relocating to Portland, Oregon in May 2026

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organization & Talent Development professional who thrives on uncovering learning needs via data analysis, developing learning experiences grounded in insights, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people development—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in data analysis to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via evidence-based {data|insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready {data|insights} on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, enhancing product understanding and driving a 78% client conversion rate
bullets.reason: Merged facilitate + execute/product training into one bullet — removes duplication, retains 600+ metric and conversion rate

bullets.text: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify {ld|training needs} and design 3 new educational programs
bullets.reason: JD explicitly requires training needs assessment; reordered to surface this second

bullets.text: Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional {collab|staff}, improving {ld|staff knowledge} and reducing {prog|onboarding} time by 75%
bullets.reason: Onboarding and staff knowledge development directly match JD duties; reordered to #3

bullets.text: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to launch 15+ educational products, refining program positioning, delivery strategy, and operations via feedback analysis
bullets.original: Execute a new product training program by hosting live sessions and producing videos that reached 500+ participants
bullets.reason: JD: participate in workgroups, represent P&C team, agency collaboration — culture-building angle fits Cascadia's mission-driven nonprofit context

bullets.text: Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal {train|training} on {dei|DEI, Inclusive Leadership}, and performance review
bullets.reason: DEI + internal training leads — Cascadia is an HSI with explicit equity values; signals DEI training alignment

bullets.text: Elevated employee experience through {ld|L&D projects} focused on engagement, rewards & recognition, and survey development
bullets.reason: L&D projects bullet leads with learning emphasis matching T&D Specialist role

bullets.text: Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming feedback data into actionable reports that informed {ld|L&D strategies}
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on {ld|L&D priorities}
Initiated a {dei|DEI} pilot with an {train|Inclusive Leadership workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
bullets.reason: DEI facilitation leads — Inclusive Leadership workshop directly shows training facilitation + Cascadia's equity mission alignment

bullets.text: Optimized {prog|onboarding} across 3 global locations by coordinating process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
bullets.reason: Onboarding support is an explicit JD duty — surfacing this second signals direct match

bullets.text: Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing cross-functional stakeholders
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Designed and {train|facilitated} a 3-week English immersion {ld|curriculum} for 12 international students, incorporating feedback-driven adjustments to continuously improve {ld|learning outcomes}
Delivered culturally responsive lessons, adjusting content and pacing in real time to meet {dei|learner needs} and maintain engagement
Developed {ld|instructional materials}—slides, worksheets, and activities—aligned to learning objectives to support knowledge retention and skill application

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

education.degree: BA in Psychology and Education Studies (GPA: 4.00)
bullets: Relevant Coursework: Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Psychological Testing, Learning and Motivation

projects: Consulting Projects: Enhanced Elanco Animal Health employer branding & talent attraction strategy via survey design/execution
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping employer attractiveness for YouTube via mixed-method research
Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition scholarship), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (Full Scholarship)

sections.title: Position Overview
sections.content: The {train|Training and Development Specialist} works within the People and Culture department to {train|develop, implement, and facilitate} live and online trainings. Assists with {prog|onboarding new staff}, administers the {tech|LMS}, and facilitates trainings supporting a wide range of staff. Person-first, {dei|trauma-informed} approach required.

items.text: Facilitate {train|in-person and virtual training} for new hires, including foundational technology systems training
items.reason: InGenius #1: "Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants" — reordered to lead

items.text: Develop and provide training: {train|DEI, Inclusive Leadership}, management, safety, EHR documentation
items.reason:
NextGen #1: "internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership" — reordered to lead
→ Vestas #1: DEI Inclusive Leadership workshop

items.text: Facilitate independently for {train|up to 8 hours (all day)}, in person or virtually
{tech|LMS administration}: user accounts, modules, training plans, auto-enrollments, reports
items.reason: Skills line: LMS (Canvas, Moodle) + MS Office & Teams — word swap adds Teams

items.text: Train staff in {tech|LMS, EHR, HRIS, Microsoft Teams}
Assess agency {ld|training needs}; develop and implement instructional material
items.reason: InGenius #2: word swap "learning gaps" → "training needs" — direct terminology match

items.text: Participate in workgroups; represent {collab|training team and P&C department}
items.reason: NextGen #2: "L&D projects" — reordered to surface learning emphasis

items.text: {prog|Assist with onboarding new staff} and support staff at all levels of the agency
Bachelor's Degree in related field preferred
2 years experience providing training or teaching required
Knowledge of {ld|training curriculum design and implementation}

sections.title: H1B Status
sections.content: 🏛️ Cap-Exempt — Cascadia Health is a nonprofit behavioral health organization; qualifies as cap-exempt employer under INA §214(g)(5).

changeLog.before: solving problems through data analysis / developing people programs
changeLog.after: designing and facilitating training programs / developing learning experiences
changeLog.why: JD core duties: training facilitation + L&D design — front-loading these in summary signals direct competency match
Facilitation is the #1 JD requirement — leading with 600+ facilitation metric signals core competency
JD explicitly requires training needs assessment; reordered to surface early
JD uses "training needs assessment" — direct terminology alignment

changeLog.after: now #3: Develop guides and workflows (staff knowledge)
changeLog.why: Onboarding and staff knowledge development directly match JD duties
JD uses "staff" throughout; "staff knowledge" matches org context (behavioral health staff)
DEI + internal training leads — Cascadia is an HSI with explicit equity values
L&D emphasis matches T&D Specialist role focus
Facilitation + DEI alignment with Cascadia's mission as an HSI
JD explicitly asks to "assist with onboarding new staff" — surfaces this skill directly

changeLog.after: HRIS · LMS (Canvas, Moodle) · MS Office & Teams
changeLog.why: JD specifically mentions LMS admin + Microsoft Teams training — LMS is core to the role; Teams signals direct tool familiarity
